output.code:
import re, json, textwrap, os
from pathlib import Path

text_path = Path("/mnt/data/file-XAG3bntAgHrjewrjqypt1Q-Q096.txt")
text = text_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

def find_reference_span(text):
    # Find (12:6-7)
    m = re.search(r"\(12:6-7\)", text)
    if m:
        return {"span_start": m.start(), "span_end": m.end(), "span_text": text[m.start():m.end()]}
    return None

def find_blockquote_spans(text):
    spans = []
    # Find occurrences of > "
    for m in re.finditer(r">\s*\"", text):
        start_quote = m.end()  # position after the opening double quote
        # find the closing quote from start_quote onward, but only up to the end of that line
        line_end = text.find("\n", start_quote)
        if line_end == -1:
            line_end = len(text)
        # find closing quote before line_end
        closing_pos = text.find("\"", start_quote, line_end)
        if closing_pos == -1:
            # no closing quote; take until end of line (exclude quotes rule already applied)
            end_idx = line_end
        else:
            end_idx = closing_pos
        spans.append({"span_start": start_quote, "span_end": end_idx, "span_text": text[start_quote:end_idx]})
    return spans

ref_span = find_reference_span(text)
quote_spans = find_blockquote_spans(text)

ref_span, quote_spans, len(quote_spans)

import json

# Using the spans computed earlier (ref_span, quote_spans)
# Build the citations list per the schema

# Prepare verse texts from file_search (we'll hardcode from the search results we saw to avoid external calls here)
ayah_12_6 = "وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَا أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰ أَبَوَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْحَاقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ"
ayah_12_7 = "لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ آيَاتٌ لِلسَّائِلِينَ"

citations = []

# Split the reference range (12:6-7) into two Aya entries using the same span for both
if ref_span:
    for aya_num, aya_text in [(6, ayah_12_6), (7, ayah_12_7)]:
        citations.append({
            "span_start": ref_span["span_start"],
            "span_end": ref_span["span_end"],
            "span_text": ref_span["span_text"],
            "citation_type": "Aya",
            "is_valid": True,
            "aya_reference": {
                "sura_name": "يوسف",
                "sura_number": 12,
                "aya_number": aya_num
            },
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": aya_text,
            "completely_invalid": False
        })

# Now the two quoted lines (treat as Aya claims but invalid)
for qs in quote_spans:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": qs["span_start"],
        "span_end": qs["span_end"],
        "span_text": qs["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

# Compose final JSON according to the response schema: a dict with key 'citations'
result = {"citations": citations}
print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

content.text: {"citations":[{"span_start":318,"span_end":326,"span_text":"(12:6-7)","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":true,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"يوسف","sura_number":12,"aya_number":6},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَا أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰ أَبَوَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْحَاقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ","completely_invalid":false},{"span_start":318,"span_end":326,"span_text":"(12:6-7)","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":true,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"يوسف","sura_number":12,"aya_number":7},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ آيَاتٌ لِلسَّائِلِينَ","completely_invalid":false},{"span_start":426,"span_end":477,"span_text":"يا هذا، هذا حلم حسن، وهو سيحكم في الأرض، ونحن نخدمه","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{ "sura_name": "", "sura_number": 0, "aya_number": 0 },"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"خطأ","completely_invalid":true},{"span_start":593,"span_end":646,"span_text":"فأعرضوا عنه، واقترعوا أن يقتلوه، أو أن يطرحوه في الجب","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{ "sura_name": "", "sura_number": 0, "aya_number": 0 },"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"خطأ","completely_invalid":true}]}

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
